answer:
**Johann Gottlieb Fichte's *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens* ("Reclaiming Freedom of Thought from the Princes of Europe," 1793) treats freedom of thought as its central topic.**[^1] The entire short pamphlet (two chapters) argues against princes' suppression of thought, asserting it as an inalienable human right essential for human progress, state welfare, and resistance to tyranny.[^2]

**Freedom of thought is also discussed, but more peripherally, in two chapters of Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation*.**
- In Volume III, Chapter 28, it is linked to religious freedom and unrestricted press in China, portraying it as minimally constrained there compared to despotic measures elsewhere.[^3]
- In Volume IV, Chapter 15, masters in Anglo-America are described as prohibiting slaves from reading to prevent freedom of thought from enabling their enlightenment or rebellion, tying it to the masters' own liberty.[^4]

No other works in the Falsafa corpus treat "freedom of thought" (or close variants) as a topic; searches returned only these hits across 37 works.[^5]

[^1]: [/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01/translation/#p-effd89](https://falsafa.com/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01/translation/#p-effd89).

[^2]: See Chapters 1–2 throughout; e.g., [/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-be3fae](https://falsafa.com/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-be3fae), [/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-8e054b](https://falsafa.com/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-8e054b).

[^3]: [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/28-des-rapports-observes-entre-les-moyens-d-existence/translation/#p-2ea79e](https://falsafa.com/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/28-des-rapports-observes-entre-les-moyens-d-existence/translation/#p-2ea79e).

[^4]: [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/15-de-l-influence-de-l-esclavage-domestique-sur-l-esp/translation/#p-5096be](https://falsafa.com/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/15-de-l-influence-de-l-esclavage-domestique-sur-l-esp/translation/#p-5096be).

[^5]: Corpus searches for "freedom of thought" (16 results, all from these works), "liberty of thought," "free thought," and "freedom to think" (0 results each).
